LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION TO ALLEGE A CLAIM FOR WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF FEHA, SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, AND OVERRULED AS TO THE THIRD, FOURTH, AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION ON THE GROUNDS OF FAILURE TO TIMELY PRESENT A GOVERNMENT CODE CLAIM. AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. IF THE ALLEGATION IS THAT MR. BESHIR WAS NOT ACTING IN THE COURSE OR SCOPE OF HIS EMPLOYMENT WHEN THE THREAT WAS MADE, THERE IS NO BASIS, AS A MATTER OF LAW, FOR A CLAIM OF INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AGAINST DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY. DEFENDANT ABDU BESHIR'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION AND OVERRULED AS TO THE FIFTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT SHOW AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT PLAINTIFF UNTIMELY FILED HIS GOVERNMENT CODE CLAIM. PLAINTIFF ACKNOWLEDGES THAT HIS FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT PROPERLY PLED, BUT IS GRANTED LEAVE TO DO SO. PLAINTIFF ALSO ACKNOWLEDGES THAT HIS SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION AND HIS SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST BESHIR ARE NOT PROPERLY PLED. ALL OF THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION CHALLENGED BY THIS DEMURRER ARE SUFFICIENTLY PLED. AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE COURT FINDS DEFENDANT'S GROUNDS ON THE BASIS OF A FAILURE TO PRESENT A TIMELY CLAIM WERE NOT FAIRLY PRESENTED IN DEFENDANT'S MOVING PAPERS WHICH WOULD HAVE ALLOWED PLAINTIFF TO PROPERLY OPPOSE. AS TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO DEFENDANT MR. BESHIR, 905.2 WOULD NOT APPLY AS MR. BESHIR WAS OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF HIS EMPLOYMENT. PLAINTIFF SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE WHICH MAY BE E-MAILED TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPO

ATTY. BRIAN J. FINN OF THE LAW OFFICES OF CHOLAKIAN & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 1-650-871-9544 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; Plaintiff Ronald Dunn's motion to disqualify Kevin Cholakian and Cholakian and Associates from representing defendants San Francisco Housing Authority and Abdu Beshir is denied. No substantial relationship exists between the earlier representation of Mr. Dunn by the Cholokian firm and their current representation of defendants per H.F. Ahmanson & Co. v. Salomon Brothers, Inc. (1991) 229 Cal.App.3d 1445. The former and current representations are factually and legally distinct even though they each involve allegations of assault. There has been an insufficient showing that Mr. Cholokian or anyone at his firm learned or could have learned any information during the course of the former representation that is material to the current actions. Nor is there anything to suggest they learned Mr. Dunn's policy or strategy related to litigation, as occurred in Knight v. Ferguson (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 1207, 1215. Order signed in open court. The Court modifies its tentative ruling, and orders as follows: Defendant San Francisco Housing Authority's demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first cause of action to allege a claim for wrongful termination in violation of FEHA, sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action, and overruled as to the third, fourth, and sixth causes of action on the grounds of failure to timely present a Government Code Claim. As to the seventh cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, the demurrer is sustained as to Defendant San Francisco Housing Authority without leave to amend. If the allegation is that Mr. Beshir was not acting in the course or scope of his employment when the threat was made, there is no basis, as a matter of law, for a claim of intentional infliction of emotional distress against Defendant San Francisco Housing Authority. Defendant Abdu Beshir's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action and overruled as to the fifth and seventh causes of action. The complaint does not show as a matter of law that plaintiff untimely filed his Government Code claim. Plaintiff acknowledges that his first cause of action is not properly pled, but is granted leave to do so. Plaintiff also acknowledges that his second cause of action and his sixth cause of action against Beshir are not properly pled. All of the other causes of action challenged by this demurrer are sufficiently pled. As to the fifth cause of action, the court finds Defendant's grounds on the basis of a failure to present a timely claim were not fairly presented in Defendant's moving papers which would have allowed Plaintiff to properly oppose. As to the 7th Cause of Action as to Defendant Mr. Beshir, 905.2 would not apply as Mr. Beshir was outside the scope of his employment. Plaintiff shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature which may be e-mailed to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Notice Of Abdu Beshir And San Francisco Housing Authoritys Motion To Dismiss Or Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Oct-23-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY AND ABDU BESHIR'S Motion To Dismiss Or Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action, the motion is denied. Art. 17 of the MOU clearly provides that "[e]ach individual covered by this MOU shall sign a letter to abide by the provision." There is no evidence that plaintiff ever signed such a letter. Thus, there is no agreement to arbitrate. The doctrine of equitable estoppel does not apply. In this case, there is no unfairness/repudiation to warrant the application of equitable estoppel. Plaintiff never agreed to arbitrate his individual claims in the first place. The fact that plaintiff's union is currently arbitrating a grievance-defined as a dispute regarding the interpretation or application of the MOU-under Art. 18 of the MOU is irrelevant. The current action does not allege a breach of the MOU. Plaintiff is acting consistently with the terms of the contract and not taking advantage of certain terms and repudiating others. See Goldman v. KPMG LLP (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 209, 220 ("if a plaintiff relies on the terms of an agreement to assert his or her claims against a nonsignatory defendant, the plaintiff may be equitably estopped from repudiating the arbitration clause of that very agreement"); Crowley Maritime Corp. v. Boston Old Colony Ins. Co. (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 1061, 1071 ("A common theme in these cases is that the party seeking relief was suing on the contract itself, not a statute or some other basis outside the contract."). ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Linda S. Forster, CSR #13286. (302/EHG)
